name: Atargatis Extended
description: Atargatis, also known as Atar'ateh, was a major goddess in northern Syria during the Hellenistic and Roman periods. She was a complex deity, embodying aspects of fertility, protection, and cosmic power. Her origins trace back to earlier Semitic goddesses, particularly related to water and agriculture. Atargatis was often depicted as a mermaid or associated with fish, reflecting her connection to bodies of water, especially the sacred lake at Hierapolis Bambyce (Manbij). She was considered a protector of cities and a source of abundance, ensuring the fertility of the land and the prosperity of her devotees. Her cult involved elaborate rituals and festivals, and her influence extended throughout the region and beyond, even influencing Roman religious practices. Atargatis's powers included control over water, fertility, and the protection of her followers. She was seen as a mother goddess figure, providing sustenance and security. Her role in Syrian mythology was central, representing the life-giving forces of nature and the enduring strength of the divine feminine. Some syncretic interpretations linked her to other goddesses like Aphrodite or Juno, further highlighting her multifaceted nature and widespread appeal. She was often accompanied by Hadad, a weather god, forming a divine couple that symbolized the balance of nature and the cycles of life.
subtitle: Syrian Goddess of Fertility and Protection

keyMyths.title: The Sacred Fish of Atargatis
keyMyths.description: The sacred lake at Hierapolis Bambyce was filled with fish considered sacred to Atargatis. These fish were not to be harmed or eaten, as they were believed to embody the goddess's power and blessings.

keyMyths.title: Atargatis and Hadad
keyMyths.description: Atargatis was often paired with Hadad, a weather god, representing the divine balance of feminine and masculine energies. Their union ensured the fertility of the land and the prosperity of the people.

keyMyths.title: The Founding of Hierapolis
keyMyths.description: Legend has it that Atargatis played a crucial role in the founding or protection of Hierapolis Bambyce. The city became a major center of her worship, attracting pilgrims from far and wide.

sources.author: Lucian of Samosata
sources.description: A detailed account of the cult of Atargatis at Hierapolis Bambyce.
